rent.summary: This is a standard residential lease agreement. Overall, the terms are fairly common for rental contracts, but there are a few clauses you should pay attention to.

clauses.title: Monthly Rent & Payment
clauses.originalText: Tenant agrees to pay $1,500 monthly, due on the 1st of each month...
clauses.simpleExplanation: You need to pay $1,500 every month. Think of it like paying for your spot to live there. If you pay late, you might have to pay extra money as a penalty.

clauses.title: Security Deposit
clauses.originalText: A security deposit equal to one month's rent shall be collected...
clauses.simpleExplanation: You pay $1,500 upfront that the landlord holds onto. It's like a safety piggy bank — if you don't break anything and pay all your rent, you get it back when you move out.

clauses.title: Late Payment Fee
clauses.originalText: A late fee of 10% shall be applied to any payment received after the 5th...
clauses.simpleExplanation: If you don't pay by the 5th of the month, you owe an extra $150. That's pretty steep! Many places only charge 5%. Try to always pay on time.

clauses.title: Maintenance Responsibilities
clauses.originalText: Tenant shall be responsible for minor repairs under $100...
clauses.simpleExplanation: If something small breaks (like a clogged drain or a broken doorknob), you have to fix it yourself if it costs less than $100. For bigger stuff, the landlord pays.

divorce.summary: This divorce agreement covers asset division, custody arrangements, and support payments. Several clauses require careful attention.

clauses.title: Asset Division
clauses.originalText: Marital assets shall be divided equally between parties...
clauses.simpleExplanation: Everything you both bought or earned while married gets split 50/50. This includes the house, cars, savings, and retirement accounts.

clauses.title: Child Custody
clauses.originalText: Joint legal custody with primary physical custody to Party A...
clauses.simpleExplanation: Both parents get to make big decisions about the kids together. But the kids will live mostly with one parent and visit the other.

clauses.title: Non-Disparagement
clauses.originalText: Neither party shall disparage the other in presence of minor children...
clauses.simpleExplanation: You can't say mean things about each other in front of the kids. This protects the children from being caught in the middle.

clauses.title: Support Modification
clauses.originalText: Support payments may only be modified through court proceedings...
clauses.simpleExplanation: If you want to change how much money is paid, you have to go back to court. You can't just agree privately and change it.

default.summary: This contract contains several standard legal provisions. We've identified the key terms and explained them in simple language below.

clauses.title: Term & Duration
clauses.originalText: This agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue...
clauses.simpleExplanation: This part tells you how long the deal lasts. After this time, the agreement ends unless you renew it.

clauses.title: Termination Rights
clauses.originalText: Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice...
clauses.simpleExplanation: If you want to end this agreement, you need to tell the other person in writing and wait 30 days.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.originalText: All information shared shall remain confidential and not be disclosed...
clauses.simpleExplanation: Whatever private information you share during this deal, the other person can't tell anyone else about it.

clauses.title: Liability Limitation
clauses.originalText: Neither party shall be liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages...
clauses.simpleExplanation: If something goes wrong, you can only sue for the actual damage caused — not for extra stuff like emotional stress.
